issues: The AI assistant overlay is more accessible than before but is still not a fully trapped modal dialog in the same way as the shared Modal component.
There is still no automated accessibility regression suite; current verification is code-based plus successful build checks.
strengths: The mobile bottom navigation now maps to real behavior by opening the AI assistant instead of leaving users on a dead tab state.
Fixed mobile surfaces are better spaced with safe-area-aware offsets, reducing collisions between the bottom nav, FAB, assistant trigger, and toast stack.
Primary mobile controls in the header, task rows, and filter area are more thumb-friendly and less cramped.
The task edit flow is more comfortable on small screens thanks to stacked form layout and full-width actions.

Accessibility improved meaningfully through semantic main content, explicit search labeling, richer task action labels, visible focus styling, better day-section disclosure semantics, and stronger modal focus restoration.
The refactor remained focused and preserved the app's existing product behavior and design direction.
Build and type-safety verification passed successfully.
